archetype: Real-Time Cryogenic Helium Inventory & Phase-Transition Density Orchestration System for Superconducting Magnet Quench Recovery in Particle Accelerators
domain: High-Energy Physics Infrastructure Management (Large Hadron Collider Scale)
description: This system manages the complex thermodynamic state of supercritical helium across multi-kilometer cryogenic distribution lines in particle accelerators, dynamically calculating mass flow rates based on real-time P-T (Pressure-Temperature) sensor fusion to prevent magnet quenches during ramp-up. It is used by cryogenic control engineers and automated safety interlocks to orchestrate the recovery of superconducting magnets after a thermal excursion, requiring sub-second latency in synthesizing control signals for helium compressors and phase separators.
rules: MUST adhere to IEC 61508 SIL 3 safety integrity levels for all logic handling quench protection interlocks and helium venting sequences.
MUST implement a custom implementation of the NIST RefProp equation of state library to calculate helium density within 0.05% accuracy at 1.9K to 4.5K operating ranges.
MUST ingest time-synchronized telemetry via IEEE 1588 PTP over deterministic Ethernet (TSN) to align data from distributed dilution refrigerators and cryopumps.
MUST enforce a 'fail-safe' architecture where any loss of sensor visibility defaults to a controlled warm-up or isolation state rather than continued operation.
MUST handle the transition between liquid and supercritical phases in real-time without phase-boundary hysteresis errors that could cause pressure surges.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use standard SQL relational databases for real-time time-series storage of high-frequency pressure transients, as write latency will miss critical thermodynamic spikes.
DO NOT rely on asynchronous event loops for the primary quench protection logic, as non-deterministic garbage collection pauses can delay critical shutdown commands by milliseconds.
DO NOT approximate helium thermodynamic properties using ideal gas laws, which fail catastrophically near the lambda point (2.17K) and lead to incorrect inventory calculations.
key_technical_challenges: Modeling the non-linear two-phase flow dynamics of superfluid helium in complex piping geometries with moving boundaries during rapid cooldown.
Synchronizing millisecond-level sensor data from hundreds of distributed nodes to reconstruct a coherent 3D density map of the cryostat without clock drift.
Predicting future pressure excursions 10-20 seconds ahead based on current compressor rotational speed and valve positions to pre-emptively adjust flow rates.

recommended_stack.language: Rust — Guarantees memory safety without a garbage collector for hard real-time constraints in safety-critical loops.
recommended_stack.framework: Tokio with custom real-time scheduler — Provides deterministic task scheduling for handling concurrent high-frequency sensor streams.
recommended_stack.database_engine: QuestDB — High-throughput time-series database optimized for ingesting millions of sensor points per second with SQL-compatible retention policies.
recommended_stack.cache: Yacl (Yet Another C++ Library) or custom Ring Buffer — For zero-copy, lock-free data sharing between the ingestion and analysis threads.
recommended_stack.message_broker: ZeroMQ — Provides low-latency, asynchronous patterns for distributing control commands to hardware PLCs without broker overhead.

compliance_flags: EPRI Guidelines for Superconducting Magnet Safety
